name: Chicken Tandoori
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Ginger, Cut Into Small Pieces
1/2 Cup Beetroot Extract (See - Radishes and Green Chile Peppers)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine beetroot extract with 1/2 cup water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer until fork tender (15-20 minutes). Drain well and reserve the liquid.
2. Cut slits into the legs and breasts of each chicken.
3. Soak saffron threads in boiling water for about 5 minutes, then brush generously over the chicken and into the slits. Let stand at room temperature for 20 minutes.
4. Puree garlic and ginger in a food processor or blender. Add the remaining ingredients and blend thoroughly.
5. Generously coat the chickens with the mixture, ensuring it reaches the slits. Cover and let stand at room temperature for 12 hours.
6. Preheat the oven to 500 degrees F (260 degrees C). Butter a shallow roasting pan and place the chickens inside. Drizzle with melted butter.
7. Roast for 10-12 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) and continue roasting until the chicken is cooked through and appears pink (25-30 minutes).
8. Arrange the chicken on lettuce leaves and garnish with lemon and lime wedges, radishes, and chile peppers.
